title: Psychotria tabacifolia
taxonomy: Psychotria tabacifolia is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises approximately 600-700 genera and 13,000 species of flowering plants. Psychotria tabacifolia is a genus of flowering plants in the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical regions. The species is further categorized within the Rubiaceae family as belonging to the Rubioideae subfamily. The specific classification of Psychotria tabacifolia may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Psychotria tabacifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the tobacco-leaf psychotria or wild tobacco. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as skin infections and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria tabacifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
